target.title: fix(doctor): warn and document QMD session recall gates
target.body:
## Summary

- Add an `openclaw doctor` warning when QMD is the active memory backend and the default agent searches `sessions`, but QMD session transcript export is not enabled.
- Clarify the session transcript recall gates in memory docs:
  - session-memory must be enabled;
  - `memorySearch.sources` must include `sessions`;
  - QMD transcript export additionally requires `memory.qmd.sessions.enabled: true`;
  - session hits still obey `tools.sessions.visibility`.
- Point users to either enable `memory.qmd.sessions.enabled` or remove `sessions` from the default agent's `memorySearch.sources` when QMD session recall is not intended.
- Cover the warning, enabled, explicit-disabled, memory-only, and combined QMD-binary-warning cases in `doctor-memory-search` tests.

## Why

Related to #53550 and supersedes #80935.

The underlying QMD session recall behavior has multiple gates that are easy to miss:

1. session-memory must be enabled;
2. `memorySearch.sources` must include `sessions`;
3. when QMD is the memory backend, transcript export must also be enabled with `memory.qmd.sessions.enabled: true`;
4. returned session hits are filtered by `tools.sessions.visibility`.

This PR keeps behavior unchanged. It adds documentation plus a diagnostic-only warning for the common QMD export mismatch. It does not enable QMD session export automatically, widen transcript visibility defaults, or change indexing/search behavior.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts` (46 passed)
- `corepack pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/concepts/memory-qmd.md docs/concepts/memory-search.md docs/reference/memory-config.md` (0 errors)
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/concepts/memory-qmd.md docs/concepts/memory-search.md docs/reference/memory-config.md` (passed)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `openclaw doctor` warns when QMD is the active memory backend, the default agent resolves `memorySearch.sources` with `sessions`, and QMD session transcript export is not enabled.
- **Real environment tested:** Disposable OpenClaw source checkout at current PR head `fe1bbb3ecad571be237e49e2173963aea557cfa1` on Linux/arm64. No live Gateway, live user config, live session store, or real secret-bearing environment was used.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** In the disposable checkout, ran the focused doctor memory-search regression for the QMD session-source warning path:

  ```bash
  node <pinned-corepack-pnpm>/bin/pnpm.mjs exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts -t "QMD backend uses session sources" --reporter=dot --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** Vitest reported:

  ```text
  Test Files 1 passed (1)
  Tests 1 passed | 53 skipped (54)
  ```

  The focused regression asserts that the doctor note contains `memorySearch.sources with sessions`, `memory.qmd.sessions.enabled is not true`, and the remediation command `openclaw config set memory.qmd.sessions.enabled true`.
- **Observed result:** The current-head doctor memory-search health contribution emits the new QMD session-transcript export warning with the affected gate and remediation path.
- **What was not tested:** Live gateway startup, live user configuration, real QMD indexing/search, and session transcript visibility were deliberately not exercised; this PR is diagnostic/docs-only and the proof is limited to the after-fix doctor warning path.

## Scope

Diagnostic and documentation only. No runtime behavior change to session transcript indexing, export, search, or visibility defaults.

target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the opt-in messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols TTS preprocessing setting.
- Applies emoji/symbol stripping through the shared speech-core preparation path before file, stream, and telephony synthesis requests.
- Returns a clear no-op failure when stripping leaves no speakable text, instead of sending an empty provider request.
- Keeps default behavior unchanged when the option is disabled or unset.
- Reviewer focus: config schema/docs, the shared TTS preprocessing path, and the empty-after-strip guard.

Out of scope: changing default TTS behavior, validating every vendor-specific pronunciation path, or adding provider-specific filtering.

## Linked context

- Related: #77969.
- This was opened as a focused external fix for noisy TTS output when emoji and decorative symbols are spoken by providers.
- No maintainer-only deployment, credential, or production environment change is required.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: optional stripping of emoji/symbol characters before TTS synthesis across file, stream, and telephony paths; empty-after-strip input is blocked before provider calls.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, branch tts-skip-v3, commit 06545b841d08, using the bundled real Microsoft speech provider path (extensions/microsoft -> node-edge-tts -> speech.platform.bing.com). No API keys or private account secrets were used.

  - node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
  - pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
- Evidence after fix:

## Tests and validation

Commands run for the behavioral change:

target.title: fix(agents): suppress EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError on user-ini…
target.body:
## Summary

When a user presses the Stop button during agent thinking, the session fence mechanism detects session file modification and throws `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`. This error is injected into the conversation context, polluting the agent's next turn with noise.

This PR identifies user-initiated aborts and suppresses the takeover error from conversation context while still logging it for debugging. The abort provenance fix ensures that **restart** and **auth-revoked** abort paths are NOT classified as user-initiated.

### Key Changes

- Add `shouldSuppressTakeoverErrorOnUserAbort()` helper function
- Add `isNonUserAbortReason()` helper to detect restart/auth-revoked/timeout abort reasons from the signal
- **`createChatAbortSignalReason`** now returns structured Error reasons for `restart` (AbortError) and `auth-revoked` (AbortError) in addition to `timeout` (TimeoutError)
- `userInitiatedAbort` predicate excludes non-user abort reasons via `isNonUserAbortReason`
- Log warning instead of `Promise.reject` for user abort case
- Session fence mechanism still effective (safety boundary preserved)

### Issue Reference

Fixes #88903

### Codex Review Response

Addresses **[P1] Gate suppression on explicit user-stop provenance** finding:
- Original logic treated all non-timeout `abortSignal.aborted` as user-initiated
- Gateway restart (`stopReason: "restart"`) and provider auth logout (`stopReason: "auth-revoked"`) use the same `AbortSignal.aborted` without a timeout reason
- Fix: `createChatAbortSignalReason` now returns structured reasons for restart and auth-revoked; `isNonUserAbortReason` checks these; `userInitiatedAbort` predicate excludes them
- Ensures restart and auth-revoked takeover errors remain visible as fatal errors

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior addressed**: Suppresses `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` from conversation context when user presses Stop button, preventing error pollution in agent's next turn. Timeout, restart, and auth-revoked aborts still surface takeover errors as fatal — only genuine user Stop-button presses trigger suppression.

**Real environment tested**: Linux (Ubuntu), Node.js 22.22, `07 Open Source/02-projects/openclaw` checkout on branch `fix/suppress-session-takeover-error-on-abort-88903`, commit 7f4d2a1df.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
# 1. Run focused abort provenance helper tests (9 scenarios)
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.abort-provenance.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose

# 2. Run session lock lifecycle tests (42 existing tests for regression)
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts --run
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.abort-provenance.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose

 ✓ isNonUserAbortReason > returns true for TimeoutError
 ✓ isNonUserAbortReason > returns true for restart AbortError
 ✓ isNonUserAbortReason > returns true for auth-revoked AbortError
 ✓ isNonUserAbortReason > returns false for undefined
 ✓ isNonUserAbortReason > returns false for plain AbortError without restart/auth-revoked message
 ✓ isNonUserAbortReason > returns false for generic Error
 ✓ shouldSuppressTakeoverErrorOnUserAbort > returns true when userInitiatedAbort and cleanupError is takeover error
 ✓ shouldSuppressTakeoverErrorOnUserAbort > returns false when not userInitiatedAbort
 ✓ shouldSuppressTakeoverErrorOnUserAbort > returns false when cleanupError is not takeover error

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  9 passed (9)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts --run

 ✓ |agents| src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts (42 tests) 432ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  42 passed (42)
```

**Observed result after fix**:
- User Stop (plain AbortError, no restart/auth-revoked message) + Takeover Error: suppressed from conversation (log.warn only), agent next turn stays clean ✓
- Timeout + Takeover Error: NOT suppressed (isNonUserAbortReason returns true for TimeoutError) ✓
- Restart + Takeover Error: NOT suppressed (isNonUserAbortReason returns true for restart AbortError) ✓
- Auth-revoked + Takeover Error: NOT suppressed (isNonUserAbortReason returns true for auth-revoked AbortError) ✓
- User Stop + Other Error: NOT suppressed (not a takeover error) ✓
- Session fence detection remains active for all abort types ✓
- All 51 tests pass (42 session-lock + 9 abort-provenance) with no regressions ✓

**What was not tested**: Live UI Stop button press in actual OpenClaw dashboard (requires running dev gateway), full agent runner workflow with real provider/model calls, multi-agent concurrent access scenarios.

---

**Related issues**: #87966, #88369, #88457, #88471, #88653 (distinct from this fix - those address false positives, this addresses UX impact of legitimate user abort)

target.title: fix(agents): verify completion delivery target
target.body:
## Summary

This PR tightens ACP/subagent completion announcement delivery accounting so a completion is only counted as visibly delivered when the delivery evidence matches the requester route.

It:
- propagates outbound payload target metadata into delivery status
- validates automatic completion delivery against provider/account/channel/thread targets
- requires exact account/thread evidence for threaded origins
- preserves target metadata on failed payload outcomes for diagnostics
- keeps the current-chat generated-media compatibility path while avoiding explicit wrong-target matches

## Behavior addressed

Completion announcements can be injected through an internal/inter-session path while the original requester is a channel/thread. Broad checks such as “visible payload exists” or “message tool sent something” can count delivery as successful even when the visible delivery evidence belongs to another route.

This change makes completion delivery proof target-aware: wrong thread, missing thread, missing account, and provider mismatches no longer satisfy delivery for a concrete requester route.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** After this patch, ACP/subagent completion delivery is counted as visibly delivered only when the delivery evidence matches the requester route. Wrong provider/account/channel/thread evidence no longer satisfies delivery for a concrete requester route.
- **Real environment tested:** Non-production isolated source checkout on current upstream `main`. No production/live Gateway runtime changes were made.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Ran the focused completion-delivery regression tests with fast test mode enabled:

     ```bash
     OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 vitest run src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts src/channels/message/send.test.ts --maxWorkers=1
     ```

  2. Ran core typecheck: `pnpm tsgo:core`.
  3. Ran formatting check on the touched files with `oxfmt --check --threads=1`.
  4. Ran `git diff --check`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Focused regression tests passed:

  ```text
  5 files passed
  261 tests passed
  ```

  Additional checks passed:

  ```text
  tsgo:core: passed
  oxfmt --check touched files: passed
  git diff --check: passed
  ```

- **Observed result:** The regression tests cover message-tool completion evidence delivered to the wrong thread, evidence missing the requester thread/account, automatic completion delivery with wrong thread, and automatic completion delivery with target metadata preserved on failure. Those cases no longer count as visible completion for the original requester route.
- **What was not tested:** No live production Gateway or live Discord proof was run. The proof is based on deterministic non-production regression tests plus type/format/diff checks; maintainers can require live threaded-channel proof or apply an explicit proof override if source-level evidence is sufficient.

## Exact steps or command run after this patch

```bash
<repo>/../workspace/.github/scripts/openclaw-heavy-run --backend auto -- \
  env OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 \
  /bin/sh pnpm exec vitest run \
  src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts \
  src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts \
  src/channels/message/send.test.ts \
  --maxWorkers=1

<repo>/../workspace/.github/scripts/openclaw-heavy-run --backend auto -- \
  /bin/sh pnpm tsgo:core

/bin/sh pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts \
  src/agents/command/delivery.ts \
  src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts \
  src/channels/message/send.ts \
  src/channels/message/send.test.ts \
  src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts \
  src/infra/outbound/deliver-types.ts

git diff --check
```

## Evidence after fix

- Focused tests: 5 files passed, 261 tests passed

target.title: fix(install): harden stdin consumers to prevent pipe corruption in curl | bash
target.body:
## Problem

When invoked via `curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash`, bash reads the script from stdin. Non-interactive subprocesses (npm, openclaw daemon, openclaw plugins) inherit stdin and can consume bytes from the script stream, causing truncated function names and hangs.

PR #82918 attempted to fix this with a top-level pipe guard, but was [closed by steipete](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82918#issuecomment-4568506465) because the detection heuristic (`BASH_SOURCE[0]` + `/dev/stdin` pipe check) was too broad and fired in `bash -c` and other non-piped modes.

## Fix

Follow steipete's guidance: harden the actual stdin consumers instead of adding a global guard.

- **Non-interactive subprocesses** (`npm install`, `openclaw daemon restart`, `openclaw plugins update`, `openclaw dashboard`): redirect stdin from `/dev/null` so they cannot consume script bytes.
- **Interactive commands** (`openclaw onboard` in bootstrap): redirect stdin from `/dev/tty` so the onboarding process gets terminal input instead of the script stream.
- **Wrapper functions** (`run_with_spinner`, `run_quiet_step`): add `< /dev/null` to all fallback paths where raw commands run without gum's stdin protection, including the success-path raw-mode fallback that was previously missed.

Interactive prompts (`prompt_choice`, `gum choose`, `openclaw doctor`, final `exec openclaw onboard`) already use `/dev/tty` and are unchanged.

This approach has no detection heuristics, no re-execution, and no behavior change for direct execution (`bash install.sh`). Each subprocess simply gets the correct stdin for its purpose.

Fixes #73814

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: non-interactive subprocesses in install.sh can steal stdin bytes when the script is piped via `curl | bash`, causing truncated function names and hangs. The `run_with_spinner` success-path raw-mode fallback (line 459) was missing `< /dev/null`, allowing a child command to consume bytes from the script stream even after detecting gum raw-mode failure.
- Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, bash 3.2 and 5.2, Node v24.2.0, openclaw main at 38030bf.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  # 1. Verified all three fallback paths in run_with_spinner are protected
  #    Line 459: "$@" < /dev/null  (raw-mode fallback, gum success path - FIXED)
  #    Line 473: "$@" < /dev/null  (raw-mode fallback, gum failure path)
  #    Line 484: "$@" < /dev/null  (non-gum fallback)

  # 2. Piped dry-run (simulates curl | bash)
  cat scripts/install.sh | bash -s -- --dry-run --install-method npm

  # 3. Direct dry-run (verifies no regression)
  bash scripts/install.sh --dry-run --install-method npm

  # 4. Stdin isolation demo
  echo "script-bytes" | bash -c 'read line; echo "consumed: $line"'
  echo "script-bytes" | bash -c 'read line < /dev/null; echo "consumed: $line"'
  ```

- Evidence after fix (terminal output):

  All `run_with_spinner` fallback paths now redirect stdin:

  ```console
  $ grep -n '"[$]@"' scripts/install.sh | grep -v 'gum.*spin\|GUM.*spin'
  459:                "$@" < /dev/null
  473:            "$@" < /dev/null
  484:    "$@" < /dev/null
  ```

  Redirect counts across install.sh:

  ```console
  $ grep -c '< /dev/null' scripts/install.sh
  10
  $ grep -c '< /dev/tty' scripts/install.sh
  3
  ```

  Piped install dry-run completes without truncation:

  ```console
  $ cat scripts/install.sh | bash -s -- --dry-run --install-method npm
  Dry run: yes
  ✓ Dry run complete (no changes made)

  $ bash scripts/install.sh --dry-run --install-method npm
  Dry run: yes
  ✓ Dry run complete (no changes made)
  ```

  Stdin isolation demo confirms the redirect works:

  ```console
  $ echo "script-bytes-in-pipe" | bash -c 'read line; echo "consumed: $line"'
  consumed: script-bytes-in-pipe
  $ echo "script-bytes-in-pipe" | bash -c 'read line < /dev/null; echo "consumed: $line"'
  consumed:
  ```

- Observed result after fix: all three direct command invocations in `run_with_spinner` now redirect stdin from `/dev/null`. The previously missed success-path raw-mode fallback (line 459) no longer allows child commands to consume bytes from the script stream during `curl | bash` execution. Both piped and direct execution modes complete the dry-run without truncation or hangs. Interactive commands (`openclaw onboard`) continue to receive `/dev/tty` for user prompts.
- What was not tested: full npm install in piped mode (requires clean environment without openclaw installed); WSL2 reproduction of the original truncation from #73814. The fix is mechanical (appending `< /dev/null` to one line) and does not depend on platform-specific behavior.

target.title: feat(telegram): opt-in interleaved progress lane
target.body:
## Summary

Add an opt-in Telegram renderer that projects reasoning text and structured runtime events into a single durable live message on the reasoning lane, matching CLI ergonomics without changing execution semantics.

**Gated on three conditions:** `toolProgressEnabled && interleavedProgress === true && hasReasoningLane` — inherits every existing group/DM/visibility gate.

## What it does

When `channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` is enabled:
- Reasoning text streams into the interleaved message with a "Thinking" header
- Tool starts append a sanitized line (tool name only, never raw args by default)
- Item/plan/approval/command/patch events append title-only summary lines
- A rolling timer (20s cadence) shows elapsed time + a wall-clock stamp while running, so a stalled lane is visible at a glance; cleared on completion
- The model's reply is delivered once as the polished answer message — it is **not** mixed into the Thinking lane
- Long turns spill into continuation messages instead of freezing at Telegram's length cap
- Falls back to the existing `pushStreamToolProgress` lane when the flag is off

## Key design choices

- **Projection-only** — no changes to prompts, model routing, tool execution, or auth
- **No raw data** — tool lines show name only; `interleavedToolArgs` is a separate opt-in for sanitized args
- **No new event contract** — consumes existing `onReasoningStream`, `onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary` callbacks unchanged
- **Reasoning + tools only; reply stays out** — the lane renders reasoning (`onReasoningStream`) and tool/event lines; the model's reply prose is **not** folded into it. Folding reply text in and stripping it back out at delivery proved unreliable — the model often drafts an answer, runs tools, then re-emits a revised one, leaving several answer-like copies of which only the last matches the polished final text. So the reply is delivered exactly once, as the answer. For the interactive backend the proxy restores real thinking on its own `onReasoningStream`, so reasoning is unaffected; a defensive final-answer strip remains for redacted-thinking backends that bridge assistant text into the reasoning lane. Tagged payloads (`<think>reason</think>answer`) keep only the in-tag reasoning, so answer prose after `</think>` never lands in the lane.
- **Single reasoning stream, delta-append** — appended as monotonic deltas (a cumulative snapshot contributes only its new suffix), so re-delivery never duplicates and the lane reads chronologically reasoning → tool → reasoning → …. The overlap fold is guarded: it folds a full-tail re-send (and full-increment duplicates) but never a short/mid-word coincidence, so a fresh fragment never loses its leading character.
- **Continuation on overflow** — as the durable message approaches Telegram's 4096-char cap the lane spills into a fresh continuation message (carrying recent context) instead of letting the draft stream stop editing and freeze the lane
- **Rate-limit-aware cadence** — the rolling "still running" timer ticks every 20s (not every few seconds) so N concurrently-streaming Thinking messages stay under Telegram's per-chat edit rate and don't draw 429s (a 429 drops the edit and stalls the lane); the line carries a wall-clock stamp so a genuine stall is obvious
- **No empty Thinking** — a turn that reaches final delivery with no reasoning/tool/status content (and no running timer) renders nothing, so the lane never emits a bare "Thinking" header
- **Plain-text transcript** — reasoning tag markers (`<think>` etc.) are unwrapped so the lane shows the model's prose, never raw tags

## Prerequisite

- **#80046** (CLI tool_use event bridge with sanitized args) — **merged 2026-05-29**; supplies the `onToolStart` lifecycle events the tool lines render. This branch should be rebased onto current `main` (which now contains it) before merge.

The renderer otherwise consumes only existing callbacks and is default-off, so it depends on no unmerged work. (On the standard `claude-cli` backend, CLI-*native* reasoning text additionally benefits from #85381's `thinking_delta`; without it the lane simply shows tool/event lines. The interactive backend restores thinking via its proxy, so reasoning appears regardless. Either way the default tool-progress lane is unaffected — this is not a hard dependency.)

## Changed files

- `extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts` — pure render helpers (gate, sanitize, header-strip, message render with length cap + no-content guard + wall-clock timer stamp, delta-append with cumulative-snapshot handling + guarded overlap fold, continuation-spill computation, defensive final-answer strip, status-line append, timer-interval constant)
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — wire callbacks through the renderer with fallback; single reasoning-stream delta append (reply prose kept out of the lane); spill into a continuation message before the length cap; suppress an empty Thinking flush at final delivery; 20s rolling-timer cadence; reasoning-tag unwrapping; clear rolling timer in `finally`
- `extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts` — `stripReasoningTagsForInterleaved` (extract only in-`<think>` reasoning, dropping answer prose after `</think>`, preserving code regions)
- `src/config/types.telegram.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` — opt-in flag schema
- Config surface docs + bundled channel metadata regeneration

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests: disabled-by-default, explicit opt-out, tool-progress-off, no-reasoning-lane, header dedup, rolling-timer lifecycle + wall-clock stamp, whitespace-collapse + length cap, delta-append no-duplication across a long cumulative stream, guarded overlap fold (no 1-char coincidence drop; full-tail re-send still folds), continuation-spill (repeated spills stay under the cap; giant-line force-advance; oversized-body tail-cap), no-content render guard, tagged-reasoning split (answer after `</think>` dropped; streaming/code cases), defensive final-answer strip
- [x] Live Telegram proof at current head (below)

## Verification

Local checks at the current head:

```
pnpm tsgo:extensions                                                 # typecheck — clean
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts   # lint — clean
npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.test.ts \
               extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.test.ts   # all green
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Opt-in Telegram interleaved progress renders reasoning plus tool/progress events in one durable Thinking message, without duplicating the final answer or freezing at Telegram message limits.

Real environment tested: Real OpenClaw Telegram gateway against Telegram, default-off feature explicitly enabled, two concurrent Telegram threads in one chat.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebuilt and ran the gateway from the PR head, enabled channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress, sent concurrent Telegram prompts that produced reasoning, tool progress, long Thinking output, and idle tool waits, then inspected Telegram messages and the gateway outbound Telegram send log.

Evidence after fix: Screenshot embedded below plus redacted outbound Telegram send-log metrics copied below: continuation msg=34783 -> msg=34786, final answer absent from every Thinking edit, zero bare Thinking sends, 20s timer edits at 04:20:15 -> 04:20:35 -> 04:20:55, and 99 outbound calls with 0 failures.

Observed result after fix: Thinking lane showed reasoning plus tool line, rolled into a continuation before the cap, sent the polished answer once as its own message, cleared empty Thinking output, and avoided Telegram 429s under the two-thread concurrent run.

What was not tested: No broad channel matrix or release package lane in this PR; focused local proof covers the Telegram renderer helpers, schema, lint, and extension typecheck.

## Live proof (current head)

Captured on the current head with the default-off feature enabled, over a **two-thread concurrent** session. The screenshot shows the visible lane; the rest is verified from the gateway's **outbound Telegram send log** — every `sendMessage` / `editMessageText` the bot issues — which shows message-level behavior a screenshot can't (message ids, edit cadence, final-state content, timing). Wire-level facts below are message-level metrics (ids, lengths, ok/timing) and contain no command/path/host content.

**Normal interleaving — reasoning + tool-use line in the Thinking lane, with the rolling timer:**

![interleaved lane](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/66da4d16b5a9467a82a65134976bc66aa956519c/proof-assets/interleaved-progress-telegram-2026-05-30-toolline.png)

**Wire-level verification (gateway send log, current head, one two-thread concurrent session):**

- **Long-message continuation** — `msg=34783` grew to the 4000-char cap and rolled into a fresh continuation `msg=34786` (carrying the next tool line) instead of freezing; clean seam, no garble.
- **No duplicate final answer** — on that long thinking-heavy turn the final answer was delivered once as its own message and is **absent from every Thinking edit** of `34783`/`34786`.
- **No empty Thinking** — **zero** bare-`Thinking`-header sends across the session.
- **20s rolling-timer cadence (rate-limit relief)** — while idling on a blocking tool, the "still running" line re-edited at **exactly 20s intervals** (04:20:15 → 04:20:35 → 04:20:55, body static at 3764 chars), keeping concurrent Thinking messages under Telegram's edit rate. The wall-clock stamp exposes any genuine stall.
- **No rate-limiting** — **99** outbound calls across the two concurrent threads, **0** failures (no `429`s, no oversized-preview stop) — the exact concurrency that previously drew 429s.

target.title: fix(gateway): fail fast on missing local probe auth
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a fail-fast in `openclaw gateway status` for the local loopback case: when `gateway.auth.mode` requires credentials but neither the persisted token nor an interactive credential can be resolved, the status command synthesises a failed probe with a clear `failureReason` instead of attempting an anonymous WebSocket connection that surfaces as a misleading `Connect: failed`.

Explicit `--url`/`--rpc-url` targets are intentionally exempt: a user pointing at a remote, forwarded, or open gateway should not be blocked by missing local-config credentials.

## Details

- `resolveAuthForTarget` resolves through the probe-surface helper. When the result has neither token nor password AND the target is the known local loopback AND `gateway.auth.mode` requires credentials, it falls back to the interactive-surface helper and propagates the failure reason.
- The fail-fast is scoped to `surface === "local" && target.kind === "localLoopback"`. Targets with `kind === "explicit"` (i.e. `--url`/`--rpc-url`) skip the check.
- Tests cover both the local fail-fast path and the explicit-target carve-out, and isolate ambient `gateway.auth.token` env in the probe-auth test to avoid cross-test contamination.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: before this patch, `openclaw gateway status` against a local config with `gateway.auth.mode: "token"` but an unresolvable token (e.g. `secrets.token.env` points at a non-existent env var) would still try an anonymous WebSocket connection and surface `Connect: failed` to the operator. The operator-actionable diagnosis ("missing required local auth") was buried under a misleading network error. After this patch, the probe is short-circuited with a `failureReason` that names the auth shape.

- **Real environment tested**: deployed `openclaw` runtime built from this PR head on a local laptop checkout at `/tmp/wt-68280` (rebased onto `upstream/main` at `8e1b0e61b0`), run against an isolated test config at `/tmp/probe-test-68280/openclaw.json` so the live mac-mini gateway and its real auth are not touched. The test config sets `gateway.auth.mode: "token"` and `gateway.secrets.token.env` to an env var that is explicitly unset in the shell.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  unset OPENCLAW_PROBE_NONEXISTENT_TEST_VAR
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/probe-test-68280/openclaw.json node dist/index.js gateway status --json | jq '.rpc | {ok, error, kind}'
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/probe-test-68280/openclaw.json node dist/index.js gateway status --url ws://127.0.0.1:18999 --json | jq '{rpc: .rpc | {ok, error, kind}, probeUrl: .gateway.probeUrl}'
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**: Captured live from the patched `node dist/index.js` runtime built from this PR head. The first scenario is the local-loopback fail-fast; the second is the explicit-`--url` carve-out.

  ```
  $ node dist/index.js gateway status --json | jq '.rpc | {ok, error, kind}'
  {
    "ok": false,
    "error": "Missing gateway auth token.",
    "kind": null
  }

  $ node dist/index.js gateway status --url ws://127.0.0.1:18999 --json | jq '{rpc: .rpc | {ok, error, kind}, probeUrl: .gateway.probeUrl}'
  {
    "rpc": {
      "ok": false,
      "error": "connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:18999",
      "kind": "connect"
    },
    "probeUrl": "ws://127.0.0.1:18999"
  }
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: in the local-loopback scenario, `rpc.kind` is `null` (no probe attempt was made) and `rpc.error` names the actionable auth diagnostic instead of a network error. In the explicit-URL scenario, `rpc.kind` is `"connect"` (the probe DID run) and the error is a real network-level `ECONNREFUSED` against the URL the operator supplied. The design boundary holds: missing local SecretRef diagnostics do not leak into the explicit-URL probe contract.

- **What was not tested**: the explicit-URL daemon `--rpc-url` flag was not exercised end-to-end in this proof. The carve-out is structural (`target.kind !== "explicit"` is the only gate) and is covered by the regression tests added in `c6c648a9cd` and `8e1b0e61b0`.

target.title: fix(agents): release session write lock if fence read throws on prompt release
target.body:
## Problem

On a long-running gateway, a single agent attempt can orphan the per-session write lock on the **live** process, wedging a channel for the full `maxHoldMs` lease (~17 min). Every interactive turn during that window fails with `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError`.

Observed in production: a Discord channel produced no replies from 00:08 until ~00:27, when the lock's lease expired and the watchdog reclaimed it. The lock payload's `pid`/`starttime` matched the live gateway process, so the stale-lock breaker correctly refused to reclaim it (it's a live holder, not a crash orphan) — only the lease TTL freed it.

## Root cause

`releaseHeldLockWithFence` (the embedded-attempt session-lock controller's release-for-prompt path, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts`) does:

```ts
const lock = heldLock;
heldLock = undefined;                                       // controller forgets it holds the lock
const fingerprint = await readSessionFileFingerprint(...);  // fs I/O — can throw
...
fenceSnapshot = await readSessionFileFenceSnapshot(...);    // fs I/O — can throw
fenceActive = true;
await lock.release();                                       // only reached if no throw above
```

`release()` is **not** in a `finally`. If either fence read rejects with a transient non-ENOENT error (e.g. `EIO`/`EMFILE` under load) after `heldLock` is cleared but before `lock.release()`, the function exits with the in-memory reference nulled and the underlying file lock never released.

`releaseForPrompt()` runs on every prompt submission (to free the lock for model I/O), so this is on a hot path. Because the controller thinks it no longer holds the lock, `dispose()` at attempt teardown sees `heldLock === undefined` and does nothing — yet the teardown still releases the in-process session-file **owner mutex**. The next attempt sails past the mutex and blocks on the orphaned **file lock**, failing every turn until the watchdog reclaims it at `maxHoldMs`.

## Fix

Wrap the fence bookkeeping in `try/finally` so the file lock is always released once `heldLock` is cleared, even if the fence reads throw. The original error still propagates — only the lock-release invariant is made exception-safe.

## Testing

- New regression test: a fence-read failure (`fs.stat` rejecting with `EIO`) during `releaseForPrompt` must still release the underlying lock. Fails before the fix (release called 0 times), passes after.
- `attempt.session-lock.test.ts`: 43 pass.
- `session-write-lock.test.ts`: 53 pass.
- Full `agents-embedded-agent` suite: 1301 pass (79 files), no regressions.
- `oxlint` + `oxfmt` clean on changed files.

target.title: fix(outbound): keep channel send durable when transcript mirror fails (#89626)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89626. The post-delivery transcript **mirror** in `deliverOutboundPayloads`
runs *after* the channel payloads are already sent. Its append was unguarded, so a
transient session-lock failure (`session file changed while embedded prompt lock was
released`) propagated out of the send. The whole send was then reported as `failed`
— discarding the fact that the channel message had already been delivered — and the
sub-agent announce layer retried, re-delivering the message up to **3 times** before
giving up.

The mirror is best-effort local bookkeeping. This change catches both thrown errors
and `{ ok: false }` results, logs a warning, and lets the already-successful channel
delivery stand. This matches the existing cron delivery path
(`mirrorDirectDeliveryIntoTranscript` in `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts`),
so it is consistent with the sibling surface rather than a one-sided patch. The append
still carries its idempotency key, so any later reconciliation stays deduplicated.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: duplicate sub-agent completion announcement (delivered up to 3x) when the post-send transcript mirror fails on the session lock.

Real environment tested: built OpenClaw source on macOS (Node 22.22.3), exercising the real `deliverOutboundPayloads` runtime path with a real channel outbound adapter and the real subsystem logger. The documented `session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released` lock contention was injected at the transcript-mirror append (env `OPENCLAW_FAULT_MIRROR_THROW=1`) so the race reproduces deterministically; everything downstream is the unmodified production delivery pipeline.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: drove the production `deliverOutboundPayloads({ channel, payloads, mirror })` once with the mirror append forced to fail, observing the channel adapter call count and the runtime log. Ran the same path against the pre-fix (unguarded) and post-fix code.

Evidence after fix (copied runtime/terminal output):

```text
# pre-fix (unguarded mirror append) + injected lock failure
[channel] matrix delivered to !session:main: "Sub-agent run b8efbcc9 complete: task finished."
[repro] deliverOutboundPayloads THREW: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released
[repro] channel send count = 1 (message already on the wire)

# post-fix (this patch) + injected lock failure
[channel] matrix delivered to !session:main: "Sub-agent run b8efbcc9 complete: task finished."
[outbound/deliver] failed to mirror outbound delivery into session transcript; channel send already succeeded: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released
[repro] deliverOutboundPayloads returned 1 result(s); did NOT throw.
[repro] channel send count = 1
```

Observed result after fix: with the lock failure injected, the channel adapter is invoked exactly once and `deliverOutboundPayloads` returns the delivered result instead of throwing; the mirror failure is downgraded to a single `[outbound/deliver]` warning. Pre-fix, the same condition throws after the message is already on the wire, which is what drives the sub-agent announce retry to re-deliver duplicates.

What was not tested: an end-to-end live Telegram + Raspberry Pi sub-agent run (no such hardware/model here). The session-lock race was injected deterministically at the mirror-append boundary rather than reproduced via natural timing.

## Supplemental checks (not a substitute for the proof above)

- `pnpm test src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts` — 95/95 pass, including 2 new regression tests that fail without this patch.
- `pnpm test` on siblings (`message`, `outbound-send-service`, `subagent-announce-delivery`) — 126/126 pass.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` + `pnpm tsgo:test:src` — exit 0; `oxfmt --check` + oxlint clean.

## Files
- `src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts` — guard the post-delivery transcript mirror append
- `src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts` — 2 regression tests + tighten the transcript mock type

target.title: [codex] Keep Codex waiting after raw reasoning progress
target.body:
## Summary
- Treat raw reasoning completion after a tool handoff as progress instead of a short completion-idle stall signal.
- Reuse the existing post-progress reply guard so long-running reasoning can continue until Codex sends a real terminal turn notification.
- Update the Codex turn-watch regression test to reproduce the stale-session sequence and prove it survives past the short guard.

## Root cause
A chilisaus task stalled because the embedded Codex app-server retired the turn after 60s of no stream events. Loki showed the last activity was rawResponseItem/completed with item type reasoning. The current OpenClaw logic armed the short completion-idle watch for that raw reasoning completion, so reasoning silence could be misclassified as a terminal stall.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Long-running Codex/chilisaus turns could be retired after 60 seconds when the last app-server notification was raw reasoning progress, leaving the delivery watcher alive but the underlying agent session stalled.
- Real environment tested: Alex's mac-mini OpenClaw production setup, Telegram-triggered chilisaus dispatch, Loki/OTel logs, and the local OpenClaw source checkout on this PR branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Queried Loki/OTel for the failed production run, observed the scheduler-side watchdog fail the stale run after the watcher fix, then ran the patched OpenClaw Codex turn-watch path locally with the focused command shown under validation.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied live output from the real OpenClaw Telegram/scheduler run after the watchdog safety net was active:

  ```text
  Scheduler | chilisaus-deliver:build-soccer-betting-model | 59s ago
  ❌ chilisaus [build-soccer-betting-model] failed
  Error: agent session stalled: no session/jsonl activity for ~77min while delivery watcher remained alive; likely app-server turn retired or stopped producing events
  ```

  Redacted Loki/OTel trace excerpt from the same real incident before this OpenClaw patch identified the app-server root cause:

  ```text
  2026-06-01 20:08:46 EDT codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for completion
  idleMs=60000 timeoutMs=60000
  lastActivityReason=notification:rawResponseItem/completed
  lastNotificationMethod=rawResponseItem/completed
  lastNotificationItemType=reasoning
  reason=turn_completion_idle_timeout
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The scheduler no longer silently reports the stale job as running; it surfaces a hard stalled-session failure. This PR addresses the upstream OpenClaw side by keeping raw reasoning progress on the longer post-progress guard instead of the short 60s completion-idle path that caused that Loki trace.
- What was not tested: The patched OpenClaw source was not deployed/restarted on the production gateway in this PR run.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The production gateway was not restarted from this session, so the live runtime proof shows the real incident and safety-net behavior; the OpenClaw app-server behavior change is validated in the focused local regression below.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The Loki/OTel trace excerpt above is the before evidence for the app-server root cause.

## Validation
- pnpm test -- extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts
- Result: 1 test file passed, 55 tests passed

target.title: fix(gateway): fail fast on missing local probe auth
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a fail-fast in `openclaw gateway status` for the local loopback case: when `gateway.auth.mode` requires credentials but neither the persisted token nor an interactive credential can be resolved, the status command synthesises a failed probe with a clear `failureReason` instead of attempting an anonymous WebSocket connection that surfaces as a misleading `Connect: failed`.

Explicit `--url`/`--rpc-url` targets are intentionally exempt: a user pointing at a remote, forwarded, or open gateway should not be blocked by missing local-config credentials.

## Details

- `resolveAuthForTarget` resolves through the probe-surface helper. When the result has neither token nor password AND the target is the known local loopback AND `gateway.auth.mode` requires credentials, it falls back to the interactive-surface helper and propagates the failure reason.
- The fail-fast is scoped to `surface === "local" && target.kind === "localLoopback"`. Targets with `kind === "explicit"` (i.e. `--url`/`--rpc-url`) skip the check.
- Tests cover both the local fail-fast path and the explicit-target carve-out, and isolate ambient `gateway.auth.token` env in the probe-auth test to avoid cross-test contamination.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: before this patch, `openclaw gateway status` against a local config with `gateway.auth.mode: "token"` but an unresolvable token (e.g. `secrets.token.env` points at a non-existent env var) would still try an anonymous WebSocket connection and surface `Connect: failed` to the operator. The operator-actionable diagnosis ("missing required local auth") was buried under a misleading network error. After this patch, the probe is short-circuited with a `failureReason` that names the auth shape.

- **Real environment tested**: deployed `openclaw` runtime built from this PR head on a local laptop checkout at `/tmp/wt-68280` (rebased onto `upstream/main` at `8e1b0e61b0`), run against an isolated test config at `/tmp/probe-test-68280/openclaw.json` so the live mac-mini gateway and its real auth are not touched. The test config sets `gateway.auth.mode: "token"` and `gateway.secrets.token.env` to an env var that is explicitly unset in the shell.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  unset OPENCLAW_PROBE_NONEXISTENT_TEST_VAR
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/probe-test-68280/openclaw.json node dist/index.js gateway status --json | jq '.rpc | {ok, error, kind}'
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/probe-test-68280/openclaw.json node dist/index.js gateway status --url ws://127.0.0.1:18999 --json | jq '{rpc: .rpc | {ok, error, kind}, probeUrl: .gateway.probeUrl}'
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**: Captured live from the patched `node dist/index.js` runtime built from this PR head. The first scenario is the local-loopback fail-fast; the second is the explicit-`--url` carve-out.

  ```
  $ node dist/index.js gateway status --json | jq '.rpc | {ok, error, kind}'
  {
    "ok": false,
    "error": "Missing gateway auth token.",
    "kind": null
  }

  $ node dist/index.js gateway status --url ws://127.0.0.1:18999 --json | jq '{rpc: .rpc | {ok, error, kind}, probeUrl: .gateway.probeUrl}'
  {
    "rpc": {
      "ok": false,
      "error": "connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:18999",
      "kind": "connect"
    },
    "probeUrl": "ws://127.0.0.1:18999"
  }
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: in the local-loopback scenario, `rpc.kind` is `null` (no probe attempt was made) and `rpc.error` names the actionable auth diagnostic instead of a network error. In the explicit-URL scenario, `rpc.kind` is `"connect"` (the probe DID run) and the error is a real network-level `ECONNREFUSED` against the URL the operator supplied. The design boundary holds: missing local SecretRef diagnostics do not leak into the explicit-URL probe contract.

- **What was not tested**: the explicit-URL daemon `--rpc-url` flag was not exercised end-to-end in this proof. The carve-out is structural (`target.kind !== "explicit"` is the only gate) and is covered by the regression tests added in `c6c648a9cd` and `8e1b0e61b0`.

target.title: feat(memory-core): optional cross-encoder rerank stage for memory search
target.body:
## Summary

OpenClaw's builtin (sqlite-vec) memory search does first-stage hybrid retrieval (dense `vec0` ∥ FTS5 BM25, fused) → temporal decay → MMR diversity, but has **no second-stage relevance reranker**. MMR reuses the frozen first-stage score and measures only lexical (Jaccard) redundancy — it cannot re-judge query↔document relevance, so a genuinely-best passage that first-stage scoring underranks is dropped before the model sees it.

This adds an **optional, vendor-neutral cross-encoder rerank stage** to the builtin backend, as a plugin-SDK capability:

- New `MemoryRerankProvider` seam: `rerank({ query, candidates, signal }) → { ref, score }[]`. Candidates are passed by **core-assigned `ref`** and the provider returns scored refs (not full results), so it cannot drop/duplicate/fabricate rows; scores are normalized `[0,1]` written to a separate `rerankScore` field (the fusion `score` stays intact for `minScore`/telemetry).
- Registered via `api.registerMemoryRerankProvider`, gated by a `memoryRerankProviders` manifest contract (mirrors the embedding-provider contract). **Single exclusive slot** — a second registering plugin is rejected.
- `MemoryManager.search` runs the reranker on the **wide pre-MMR pool**, then MMR diversifies, then `minScore`/trim. **Fail-open**: an aborting deadline wraps the call; on timeout/error it returns the pre-rerank order.
- **Observable, not fail-silent:** `status().custom.rerank` / the search `debug.rerank` field reports `disabled` (no reranker) / `active` (scores applied) / `degraded` (registered but returned no scores — failing, timed-out, or plugin-disabled), with a latched warn on the active→degraded transition.

**No new core config** — opt-in is registration-based. Core ships **no concrete reranker**; a TEI/`bge-reranker` implementation is a separate plugin.

Intentionally out of scope: the concrete reranker plugin; the **qmd** backend (it delegates ranking to its own server-side reranker — noted inline at the insertion point and in docs).

Reviewers should focus on: the fail-open path in `MemoryManager.search` (must never throw), the `MemoryRerankProvider` refs→scores contract, the `memoryRerankProviders` gate, and the `disabled/active/degraded` observability.

## Linked context

Closes #89477.
Related #82985.

**Supersedes #82985** (the `engineCandidates` approach): reranking belongs in a dedicated stage on the wide pre-MMR pool, not bolted onto the post-MMR corpus-supplement contract (a supplement can only reorder the already-diversified, already-trimmed top-N). This PR and #82985 overlap on `src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-runtime-core.ts` and `src/plugins/memory-state.ts`, so they should not both land — suggest closing #82985 (and its issue #82984) in favor of this seam once the approach is accepted.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? — Proposed via RFC issue #89477 (RFC-first); awaiting maintainer buy-in, hence draft.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** memory search gains an optional cross-encoder rerank stage before MMR; with no reranker registered, results and logs are unchanged. Observability honestly distinguishes active/degraded/disabled.
- **Real environment tested:** OpenClaw built from this branch, run in an **isolated** home (`OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp` throwaway — production gateway untouched; verified `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` mtime unchanged before/after). A companion plugin implementing `MemoryRerankProvider` registered against a live TEI **bge-reranker-v2-m3** cross-encoder (HTTP `/rerank`); **bge-m3** for base embeddings; a local OpenAI-compatible **Qwen3.6-27B** drove the `agent --local` turn. All endpoints self-hosted on a tailnet; no cloud services.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** indexed a 4-doc corpus, then `openclaw agent --local --json` invoking `memory_search` (corpus=all) with the reranker active, then again with the plugin's kill-switch on.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - Plugin registered in-process: `memory-reranker-tei: register at … url=…:8884 …; warmup OK`.
  - **ACTIVE** — query *"how to disable reranking without restarting the service"*; the cross-encoder elevated the correct note to #0:
    ```
    debug: {"backend":"builtin","rerank":"active","searchMs":327,"hits":4}
    #0 memory/killswitch.md  rerankScore=0.18952  score=0.43797   ← reranker's top pick
    #1 memory/pipeline.md    rerankScore=0.00069  score=0.31210
    #2 memory/infra.md       rerankScore=0.00059  score=0.32281
    #3 memory/embeddings.md  rerankScore=0.00002  score=0.21677
    ```
  - **DEGRADED** (kill-switch on) — same query:
    ```
    debug: {"backend":"builtin","rerank":"degraded","searchMs":156,"hits":4}
    warn: "rerank degraded — reranker returned no scores. Falling back to pre-rerank order. Further duplicate warnings suppressed."
    (no rerankScore on any result; pre-rerank order: killswitch, infra, pipeline, embeddings)
    ```
- **Observed result after fix:** an active reranker measurably reorders toward relevance (and sets `rerankScore`); a registered-but-not-working reranker is honestly surfaced as `"degraded"` with fail-open pre-rerank order — not a false `"active"`. With no reranker registered, behavior is unchanged. `searchMs` 327 (TEI round-trip) vs 156 (no rerank traffic).
- **What was not tested:** the qmd backend (out of scope — it has its own server-side reranker).
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** run via an isolated dev home + local tailnet model, not the production gateway.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?
- `pnpm tsgo:all` → exit 0 (whole monorepo; note `tsgo:core` excludes `extensions/`)

target.title: fix(agents): dedupe transcript rewrite suffix replay
target.body:
## Summary

Context-overflow recovery re-runs transcript suffix replay without a replay-identity guard, so byte-identical `role=user` entries are re-appended on every recovery pass and the persisted session JSONL accumulates duplicate messages (issue #66443).

- Collapses **only the proven overflow-clone pattern** during replay: byte-identical `role=user` messages and byte-identical compactions. Exact suffix history is preserved for assistant, tool-result, and custom entries.
- A legitimately repeated user message carries a **distinct timestamp**, so the full-payload hash keeps it while collapsing the identical recovery clone; compaction identity folds in the remapped `firstKeptEntryId` so two distinct compactions are not merged.
- Intended outcome: overflow recovery becomes idempotent for the clone pattern without violating the rewrite's "preserve exact suffix history" contract for legitimate entries.
- Intentionally out of scope: the broader duplicate-transcript umbrella, and repeated-bootstrap-custom dedup (custom entries are deliberately left untouched to avoid dropping legitimate history).
- Reviewers should focus on the user/compaction scoping and the timestamp-based clone-vs-repeat distinction.

## Linked context

Closes #66443

Related #79150 — superseded prior attempt; this PR addresses its "include the kept boundary in compaction identity" review note.

Not maintainer-requested; selected from the `clawsweeper:queueable-fix` backlog.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Overflow recovery duplicated `role=user` entries in the persisted session JSONL (issue #66443 captured one unique message stored as three byte-identical copies).
- Real environment tested: Linux / Node 24, exercising the actual production functions `rewriteTranscriptEntriesInSessionManager` and `rewriteTranscriptEntriesInState` against a real in-memory `SessionManager` and persisted transcript-file-state — these functions are run for real, not mocked.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/transcript-rewrite.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied terminal output. The regression reproduces the bug on unfixed `main` and passes after the fix, and added tests prove legitimate repeats survive:

```
unfixed main: × dedupes byte-identical replayed messages ...
              AssertionError: expected 2 to be 1   (the clone is re-appended)

with the fix: Test Files  1 passed (1)
              Tests       10 passed (10)           (clone collapsed; legitimate repeats kept)
```

- Observed result after fix: after the overflow-style suffix replay, a byte-identical `role=user` clone is collapsed to one entry, while (a) two user messages with distinct timestamps, (b) byte-identical repeated assistant/tool-result entries, and (c) two distinct compactions are all preserved.
- What was not tested: a live gateway context-overflow on a running OpenClaw deployment (see limitations).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this is unit/integration-level proof against the real production functions, which CONTRIBUTING.md treats as supplemental rather than a live-setup behavior capture. I could not drive a live gateway into context overflow in this environment (it needs a full running OpenClaw + provider + a forced overflow, and the sandbox network blocked both a clean install and the `crabbox check:changed` gate). A maintainer with a live setup can confirm end-to-end behavior, or apply `proof: override` for this logic-only dedup fix; I am happy to add a live capture if someone can reproduce an overflow.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the failing assertion above (`expected 2 to be 1`) is the before-state captured from unfixed `main`.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/transcript-rewrite.test.ts` -> 10 passed (4 new).
- Sibling callers: `tool-result-truncation`, `context-engine-maintenance`, `compact.hooks`, `cli-runner.context-engine` -> 126 passed.
- `tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json` and core test types: no errors in the changed files (two unrelated pre-existing errors in `src/config/io.ts` and `src/secrets/config-io.ts` are present on `main`).
- Regression coverage added in `transcript-rewrite.test.ts`: clone collapse (fails first with `expected 2 to be 1`, passes after); distinct compactions preserved; legitimate repeated user messages (distinct timestamps) preserved; byte-identical repeated assistant/tool-result entries preserved.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? No — internal transcript maintenance that removes only byte-identical recovery clones.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.
- Highest-risk area: collapsing genuinely distinct entries during replay (the `merge-risk: session-state` concern).
- How is that risk mitigated? Dedup is constrained to `role=user` messages and compactions only; the full-payload hash (including timestamp) distinguishes a recovery clone from a legitimate repeat; compaction identity includes the remapped `firstKeptEntryId`; assistant/tool-result/custom entries are never deduped. Covered by the four regression tests above.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review / ClawSweeper re-review (auto-triggered by this push).
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 "Preserve distinct replayed transcript entries": constrained the replay dedup to the proven user-message + compaction clone pattern (per the recommended "Constrain Replay Dedupe" option), restored exact-history for assistant/tool/custom entries, and added regression coverage proving legitimate repeated messages/tool entries survive. The dedup helper is shared by both the SessionManager and TranscriptFileState rewrite paths.
- Still open: real behavior proof is unit-level only; a live-overflow capture needs a real setup or a maintainer `proof: override`.

target.title: feat(compaction): add identifier survival validation after summarization
target.body:
## Summary

Improve compaction identifier survival with transcript-wide extraction and quality-guard retry hints. Fixes two security/correctness issues found during review:

**Fix 1 — Sanitize string-encoded JSON arguments before extraction**: `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers` now parses string-encoded tool arguments as JSON and passes the parsed object through `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload` before identifier scanning. Previously, string arguments (e.g. OpenAI's JSON-stringified format) were appended raw, allowing credential values like `{"token":"deadbeef12345678"}` to feed the hex identifier extractor and leak into `## Exact identifiers`.

**Fix 2 — Fail strict compaction when cap recovery cannot fit**: When strict mode detects that final capping removed required identifiers and the recovery block would exceed `MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS`, compaction is now cancelled instead of returning a summary with known identifier loss. This prevents the session from continuing in a state where strict-required identifiers are silently dropped.

## Changed files

- `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard-quality.ts` — Parse string args as JSON, sanitize via `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload` before appending; non-JSON strings still pass through
- `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts` — Cancel compaction (return `{ cancel: true }` with reason) when strict cap recovery overflows instead of silently skipping
- `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts` — Three regression tests: string-encoded JSON credential sanitization, non-JSON passthrough, strict-mode cap overflow cancel

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Two security/correctness issues in compaction identifier survival. (1) `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers` appended string-encoded tool arguments raw, allowing credential values like `{"token":"deadbeef12345678"}` to feed the hex identifier extractor and leak into `## Exact identifiers`. (2) When strict mode detected that final capping removed required identifiers and the recovery block would exceed `MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS`, the summary was returned with known identifier loss instead of cancelling compaction.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5 arm64, Node v22.19.0, pnpm v11.2.2. Built from source on branch `compaction-identifier-survival-validation` at `/tmp/wt-75336`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

Step 1. Create proof script exercising production `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers`, `extractOpaqueIdentifiers`, `extractIdentifiersFromMessages`, `computeLostIdentifiers`, and `capCompactionSummary`:

```typescript
import {
  extractMessageTextForIdentifiers,
  extractOpaqueIdentifiers,
  extractIdentifiersFromMessages,
  computeLostIdentifiers,
} from "./src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard-quality.js";
import { testing } from "./src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.js";
const { capCompactionSummary, MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS } = testing;
```

Step 2. Validate Fix 1, string-encoded JSON args with credential `token` field:

```typescript
const msg = {
  role: "assistant",
  content: [{
    type: "functionCall", id: "call_1", name: "configure",
    arguments: '{"token":"deadbeef12345678","endpoint":"https://api.acme.io/v2"}',
  }],
};
const text = extractMessageTextForIdentifiers(msg);
assert(!text.includes("deadbeef12345678"));    // credential redacted
assert(text.includes("https://api.acme.io/v2")); // non-credential survives
```

Step 3. Validate Fix 2, capping loses identifiers and recovery overflows:

```typescript
const transcriptIds = extractIdentifiersFromMessages([
  { role: "user", content: "deploy hash a1b2c3d4e5f6" },
]);
const oversized = "## Decisions\nKeep flow.\n## Exact identifiers\n"
  + "x".repeat(MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS) + "\na1b2c3d4e5f6";
const capped = capCompactionSummary(oversized);
const lost = computeLostIdentifiers(transcriptIds, capped);
assert(lost.length > 0);  // identifier at tail truncated by capping
const recovered = `${capped}\n\n## Exact identifiers (recovered)\n- ${lost[0]}`;
assert(recovered.length > MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS); // recovery overflows
```

Step 4. Run `npx tsx proof-75336.ts` from the patched branch.

Evidence after fix:

```console
$ cd /tmp/wt-75336 && npx tsx proof-75336.ts

[Proof 1] String-encoded JSON args sanitization
  PASS credential token value is redacted from string args
  PASS non-credential endpoint survives sanitization
  PASS hex identifier extractor does not capture redacted credential
  PASS non-JSON string args pass through for extraction

[Proof 2] Identifier loss detection after capping
  PASS transcript identifiers extracted from messages
  PASS hex identifier normalized to uppercase
  PASS capped summary respects size limit
  PASS identifier at tail is lost after capping
  PASS recovery block exceeds cap (triggers cancel in strict mode)

Results: 9 passed, 0 failed
```

Observed result after fix:

Fix 1: `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers` no longer leaks credential-field values from string-encoded JSON arguments. The `token` field value `deadbeef12345678` is redacted by `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload` before the hex identifier extractor can capture it. Non-credential fields and non-JSON strings pass through normally.

Fix 2: When strict mode detects identifier loss after capping and the recovery block would exceed `MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS` (16,000 chars), compaction is cancelled with a diagnostic reason instead of returning a summary with known missing identifiers. The cancel reason is stored via `setCompactionSafeguardCancelReason` for downstream diagnostics.

What was not tested: Live compaction with a real model and conversation history (requires a running OpenClaw session with enough context to trigger compaction).

target.title: fix(cron): skip deleteAfterRun and preserve counters for manual runs (#83538)
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw cron run` (manual/debug runs) on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes the job on success — consuming the one-shot and preventing its scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters, interfering with scheduled retry/backoff state.

This PR adds an `isManual` flag to `applyJobResult` so manual runs preserve the job and leave scheduled execution state untouched.

## Root cause

`applyJobResult()` has no concept of manual vs scheduled runs. When a manual run succeeds on a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job, `shouldDelete` evaluates to `true` and the job is removed from the store — the scheduled fire never happens.

Additionally, manual error/skipped/success outcomes unconditionally mutate `consecutiveErrors` and `consecutiveSkipped` counters, which control retry backoff and auto-disable behavior for scheduled runs.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `timer.ts` | Add `isManual` option to `applyJobResult`; skip `deleteAfterRun` for manual runs; guard all counter mutations and alert emissions with `!isManual` |
| `ops.ts` | Pass `isManual: true` from manual run completion and invalid-persisted-run handling |
| `timer.regression.test.ts` | 5 new tests: manual ok/error preserve job, manual doesn't increment/reset counters |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts
 ✓ cron (45 tests) 144ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  45 passed (45)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes it, preventing scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate scheduled retry counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `aa92a4a5b0`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.

### Source-level proof

```
Main branch applyJobResult():
  - shouldDelete = kind === "at" && deleteAfterRun && status === "ok"
    → no manual discriminator, always deletes on success
  - consecutiveErrors incremented unconditionally on error
  - consecutiveSkipped incremented unconditionally on skip

Fix branch:
  - shouldDelete = wouldDelete && !opts?.isManual
    → manual runs never trigger deletion
  - Counter mutations guarded: if (!opts?.isManual) { ... }
    → manual runs don't affect retry/backoff state
```

### Regression test coverage

```
✅ manual ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted
✅ manual error on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted  
✅ scheduled ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job deleted (unchanged behavior)
✅ manual error does not increment consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
✅ manual success does not reset consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
```

- **What was not tested**: Live `openclaw cron run` CLI flow (requires a running gateway with a configured `deleteAfterRun` at-job). The `isManual` flag propagation from `ops.ts` to `applyJobResult` is validated at the unit level.

Closes #83538


### Re-verification (2026-05-20)

Local checkout at `d89f14de26`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

 ✓ cron  ../../src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (39 tests) 116ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  39 passed (39)
   Duration  152ms
```

Full cron suite (`src/cron/`) also run: 94 test files, 901 passed. The 7 failures are in unrelated files (`run-log.test.ts`, `service.failure-alert.test.ts`) pre-existing on upstream/main.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `openclaw cron run` (manual) of a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job incorrectly deletes the job. Manual runs also pollute `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `0cec2acc7a`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On `upstream/main`, counter mutations and `shouldDelete` run unconditionally — no `isManual` discriminator exists.
- **Exact steps after patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts` + `src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: 46/46 timer regression tests pass (including 7 new #83538/#83933-specific tests). 7/7 failure-alert tests pass.
- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI flow; the tests exercise the same `applyJobResult` code path the Gateway calls internally.

### Key test results

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun + success | NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun + success | Deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |
| Manual error → failure alert | NOT triggered ✅ |
| Scheduled error → failure alert | Triggered ✅ |


## Real behavior proof (Docker, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker container, `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched source at `885d605e56`.

### Latest fixes

| Issue | Problem | Fix |
|-------|---------|-----|
| P1 | Manual at-job error was disabling job (`enabled=false`, `nextRunAtMs=undefined`) | At-job error retry/disable block now guarded with `if (opts?.isManual)` |
| P2 | Manual success was clearing `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown | Cooldown clearing now inside `if (!opts?.isManual)` guard |

### Test results

| Test Suite | Result |
|------------|--------|
| Timer regression (49 tests) | ✅ All passed |
| Failure-alert (7 tests) | ✅ All passed |

### Key verified behaviors

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual at-job error | `enabled` stays true, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅ |
| Manual success | `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown preserved ✅ |
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun | Job NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun | Job deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |


## Real CLI proof (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway at `885d605e56` running with `--allow-unconfigured`.

**Terminal transcript**: `openclaw cron add` → `cron show` → `cron run` (manual, triggers error) → `cron show` (job still exists, enabled=true, nextRunAtMs preserved) → `cron list` (job present) → `cron runs` (run history with error status, nextRunAtMs unchanged).

| Step | Command | Result |
|------|---------|--------|
| Add job | `openclaw cron add --name proof-job --at ... --delete-after-run` | Created with `deleteAfterRun: true` |
| Manual run | `openclaw cron run <id>` | `{"ok": true, "enqueued": true}` |
| After run | `openclaw cron show <id>` | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` ✅ |
| Job exists | `openclaw cron list` | Job still listed ✅ |
| Run history | `openclaw cron runs --id <id>` | `status: "error"`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` ✅ |


## Real CLI proof at head SHA `642c3450d9` (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway built from PR head.

| Test | Command | Before (main) | After (PR head) |
|------|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| A: at-job deleteAfterRun | `cron run` → `cron show` | Job deleted/disabled | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅ |
| B: every-job manual error | `cron run` → `cron show` | `nextRunAtMs` rewritten by backoff | `nextRunAtMs` unchanged ✅ |
| A+B: counters | `cron runs` | `consecutiveErrors` mutated | Unchanged ✅ |

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` no longer consumes `deleteAfterRun` one-shot jobs, and no longer perturbs retry counters, at-job retry/disable state, recurring backoff, or failure-alert cooldown.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container built from PR HEAD `3d32c9baff`, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.14.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `applyJobResult` has no manual-run discriminator — manual runs unconditionally drive `deleteAfterRun`, counter mutations, at-job disable, recurring backoff, and alert cooldown. The 12 regression tests added by this PR do not exist on main.

- **Exact steps or commands run after this patch**:

```bash
# Build Docker image from PR HEAD
docker build --target build -t openclaw-pr83933:test .

# Test 1: Timer regression tests (includes all 12 manual-run isolation tests)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

# Test 2: Failure alert tests (adapted for manual-run isolation)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts

# Test 3: Lint check
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test npx oxlint src/cron/service/timer.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.ts

# Test 4: Full cron test suite
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

Test 1 — Timer regression: **55 tests passed** (284ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (55 tests) 284ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  55 passed (55)
```

Test 2 — Failure alert: **10 tests passed** (63ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (10 tests) 63ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
```

Test 3 — Lint: **0 errors, 0 warnings**
```
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 13ms on 4 files with 189 rules using 10 threads.
```

Test 4 — Full cron suite: **1000 tests passed** across 98 test files
```
 Test Files  1 failed | 97 passed (98)
      Tests  2 failed | 1000 passed (1002)
```
The 2 failures are in `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts` — these look for Swift source files (`apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/CronModels.swift`) which are not present in the Docker build target. These failures are pre-existing and unrelated to this PR.

- **Observed result after fix**: All 12 new regression tests pass, confirming that manual runs preserve one-shot jobs (`deleteAfterRun`), do not increment/reset counters, do not disable at-jobs on error, do not rewrite recurring `nextRunAtMs` with backoff, and do not clear failure-alert cooldown. Control-group tests confirm scheduled runs still drive all 5 state effects normally.

- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI `openclaw cron run` end-to-end (covered by earlier Docker CLI proof at `642c3450d9` — subsequent commits were formatting-only changes via oxfmt). The `cron-protocol-conformance` tests require macOS app source and cannot run in Docker.


## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `6bc3b9e374`, 2026-05-30)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` no longer consumes `deleteAfterRun` one-shot jobs, and no longer perturbs retry counters, at-job retry/disable state, recurring backoff, failure-alert cooldown, or skipped-run alert counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container built from PR HEAD `6bc3b9e374`, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.14.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `applyJobResult` has no manual-run discriminator — manual runs unconditionally drive all 5 scheduled-state effects plus skipped-run alert paths. The 14 regression tests added by this PR do not exist on main.

- **Exact steps or commands run after this patch**:

```bash

docker build --target build -t openclaw-pr83933:test .

# Test 1: Timer regression tests (all 14 manual-run isolation tests)

# Test 4: Full cron test suite
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

Test 1 — Timer regression: **56 tests passed** (234ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (56 tests) 234ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  56 passed (56)
```

Test 2 — Failure alert: **11 tests passed** (92ms)

```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (11 tests) 92ms

      Tests  11 passed (11)
```

Test 3 — Lint: **0 errors, 0 warnings**
