```
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 10ms on 4 files with 189 rules using 10 threads.
```

Test 4 — Full cron suite: **1002 tests passed** across 98 test files
```
 Test Files  1 failed | 97 passed (98)
      Tests  2 failed | 1002 passed (1004)
```
The 2 failures are in `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts` — these look for Swift source files (`apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/CronModels.swift`) which are not present in the Docker build target. Pre-existing and unrelated to this PR.

- **Observed result after fix**: All 14 new regression tests pass, covering all 6 guarded state effects including the newly-added manual skipped-run isolation:
  - Manual `deleteAfterRun` at-job → NOT deleted ✅
  - Manual error → counters NOT incremented ✅
  - Manual success → counters NOT reset, cooldown NOT cleared ✅
  - Manual at-job error → NOT disabled, nextRunAtMs preserved ✅
  - Manual every-job error → backoff NOT applied, nextRunAtMs preserved ✅
  - Manual skipped at-job → consecutiveSkipped NOT incremented, alert NOT fired, all state preserved ✅
  - Manual skipped run with `includeSkipped: true` → failure alert NOT emitted ✅

  Control-group tests confirm scheduled runs still drive all state effects normally.

- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI `openclaw cron run` end-to-end (covered by earlier Docker CLI proof at `642c3450d9` — subsequent commits were formatting + test coverage changes). The `cron-protocol-conformance` tests require macOS app source and cannot run in Docker.

### Manual-run state contract

`cron run` is a debug/verification command. Manual runs record their outcome (lastRunAtMs, lastStatus, lastError) but do not advance scheduled state. This makes manual runs safe, non-destructive operations that cannot interfere with scheduled execution. ## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `8596607fca`, 2026-06-02 — backoff fix)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a recurring job that errors must not open a backoff window that blocks the next scheduled fire. Previously, `isErrorBackoffPending()`, `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()`, `isPendingErrorBackoffSlot()`, and `recomputeJobNextRunAtMs()` read `lastRunStatus` and `lastRunAtMs` without checking `lastRunWasManual`, causing a manual error to push `nextRunAtMs` forward and block the timer-scheduled fire.

- **Real environment tested**: Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (linux/arm64), Node.js `v24.16.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`, patched OpenClaw at commit `8596607fca`.

- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` does not check `lastRunWasManual`. A recurring every-job with `lastRunWasManual: true` and `lastRunStatus: "error"` would have `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` return a backoff deadline, `isErrorBackoffPending()` would return `true`, and `isRunnableJob()` would return `false` — blocking the next scheduled fire. `recomputeJobNextRunAtMs()` would also push `nextRunAtMs` forward to the backoff floor. Test file authored as part of this PR.

target.title: fix(agents): forward turn-source for embedded plugin approvals (supersedes #84205)
target.body:
## Summary

Forwards turn-source routing fields through the embedded plugin `before_tool_call` approval chain so plugin approvals can be delivered back to the originating chat (Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.). Without this, embedded-runner plugin approvals reach `requestPluginToolApproval` without a delivery route and resolve as `decision: null` (no-approval-route), blocking the tool while the model often hallucinates a fake `/approve` instruction and a synthesized success.

Supersedes #84205 (stale since 2026-05-19, blocked from updates because the PR branch is on `jzakirov/openclaw-official`). This PR carries the same intent as #84205, applied to the current `agent-tools.*` file layout (the upstream rename of `pi-tools.*` -> `agent-tools.*` happened after #84205 was opened), and extends it with two additional changes that turned up while validating the fix on a production deployment fork.

After ClawSweeper review the workshop fallback was tightened from `unavailableBehavior: "allow"` to the default fail-closed contract so mutating skill_workshop actions (`apply`, `reject`, `quarantine`) cannot bypass the operator-intended pending-approval default when no approval route is available.

## Details

Four commits, ordered by dependency:

1. `f5ef9d2b6f` "Allow skill workshop lifecycle without approval route" - extends the embedded-mode hook context with `turnSource*` fields and threads them through `requestPluginToolApproval`.
2. `713649e11e` "thread turn-source through embedded plugin approval contexts" - propagates `messageProvider` / `currentChannelId` / `agentAccountId` / `currentThreadTs` onto the embedded plugin `before_tool_call` hook context from every embedded entry point (codex app-server dynamic-tools and run-attempt, CLI runner, embedded-agent-runner, openclaw-tools, gateway tools-invoke-shared).
3. `5734dad75e` "thread turn-source through createOpenClawCodingTools factory" - threads the same fields through the factory that produces the HookContext consumed by every downstream plugin approval request. Without this third commit the call-site threading from commit 2 has no effect.
4. `ef5bb8d408` "block skill_workshop lifecycle approvals when no route is available" - removes the `unavailableBehavior: "allow"` carve-out per ClawSweeper P1 review. The default fail-closed contract now applies; the model sees an immediate `Plugin approval unavailable` block instead of a synthesized ALLOW_ONCE.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: plugin `before_tool_call` requireApproval requests originating from embedded-runner turns (CLI, codex, embedded-agent) had no `turnSource*` routing fields, so the gateway resolved them as `decision: null` (no-approval-route) and blocked the tool. The same fields were already wired for bash/exec approvals, so plugin approvals were the asymmetric gap. Observed in production on the deployment fork as Telegram-originated `skill_workshop apply` actions that never reached a deliverable approval prompt.

- **Real environment tested**: deployed `openclaw` runtime built from this PR head on a local laptop checkout at `/tmp/turnsource-pr`, branch `fix/embedded-plugin-approval-turn-source-superset` rebased on `upstream/main` at `e59e65be67`. Build commit `ef5bb8d408`. Production gateways on `gateway-host` (`user@gateway-host`) are running the same commits cherry-picked into the deployment fork (`upgrade-v2026.6.2` at `3ef6591a4e`) and were verified at deploy time.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  cd /tmp/turnsource-pr
  pnpm build
  grep -c "turnSourceChannel" dist/*.js
  grep -F "Plugin approval unavailable" dist/agent-tools.before-tool-call-*.js
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts -t "skill_workshop"
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**: Captured live from the patched `dist/` bundle built from this PR head.

  ```
  $ git log --oneline -4
  ef5bb8d408 fix(skills): block skill_workshop lifecycle approvals when no route is available
  5734dad75e wip(agents): thread turn-source through createOpenClawCodingTools factory
  713649e11e wip(agents): thread turn-source through embedded plugin approval contexts
  f5ef9d2b6f Allow skill workshop lifecycle without approval route

  $ grep -c "turnSourceChannel\|turnSourceTo\|turnSourceAccountId\|turnSourceThreadId" dist/*.js | grep -v ":0" | head -10
  dist/agent-command-CIibMkGi.js:4
  dist/agent-CnqqLDfu.js:12
  dist/agent-delivery-CAkfTZyN.js:9
  dist/agent-tools.before-tool-call-C4q8C-bA.js:4
  dist/agent-tools-BvNXDsuT.js:4
  dist/approval-delivery-helpers-BCbYC_RR.js:1
  dist/approval-native-brfCkLin.js:4
  dist/approval-native-BI79BlFD.js:6
  dist/approval-native-route-coordinator-C4wbDCvi.js:5

  $ grep -F "Plugin approval unavailable" dist/agent-tools.before-tool-call-*.js
  dist/agent-tools.before-tool-call-C4q8C-bA.js:				reason: "Plugin approval unavailable (no approval route)",

  $ grep -A6 "function resolveSkillWorkshopToolApproval" dist/agent-tools.before-tool-call-*.js
  function resolveSkillWorkshopToolApproval(params) {
    if (params.toolName !== "skill_workshop") return;
    const action = readLifecycleAction(params.toolParams);
    if (!action) return;
    if (resolveSkillWorkshopConfig(params.config).approvalPolicy === "auto") return;

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts -t "skill_workshop"
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  2 passed | 22 skipped (24)
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: (1) turnSource* fields propagate into the compiled embedded-runner bundle across 10+ files; (2) `resolveSkillWorkshopToolApproval` in the compiled bundle no longer emits `unavailableBehavior: "allow"`, so a no-route approval for `skill_workshop apply/reject/quarantine` falls through to the default fail-closed branch (`blocked: true, deniedReason: "plugin-approval", reason: "Plugin approval unavailable (no approval route)"`); (3) the regression test on the post-fix branch passes both the original `reasoning` round-trip and the new `blocks skill_workshop lifecycle approvals when no route is available` assertion.

- **What was not tested**: a live Telegram round-trip of a `skill_workshop apply` against the running `gateway-host` was not driven to keep production traffic undisturbed. The compiled `dist/` bundle confirms the runtime code matches the source, and the fail-closed change is conservative (a stricter contract than the previous behavior), so the deployment risk is bounded to "skill_workshop runs without a live approval route now fail-fast instead of synthesizing ALLOW_ONCE".

target.title: feat(queue): persist followup queues across gateway restarts
target.body:
## Problem

When the gateway process restarts, followup messages queued during an active agent turn are silently lost.

## Solution

Persist followup queues in shared SQLite (`followup_queue_entries` on `openclaw.sqlite`) with doctor migration for legacy `live-chat-followup-queues.json`. Persist routing, provenance, and inbound turn context (`currentInboundEventKind`, `currentInboundAudio`, `currentInboundContext`) across restarts.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Queued followup messages survive a gateway restart and redeliver exactly once with Telegram routing, reply target, input provenance, and inbound turn context (room events, quoted replies, bare resets) intact.
- Real environment tested: Linux dev checkout (`jewseppi/openclaw`, branch `feat/queue-followup-persistence`), Node 24, real temp state dir via `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, real on-disk `openclaw.sqlite` and legacy JSON sidecar I/O (persist/restore/migration modules, not in-memory mocks).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  cd openclaw
  export OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
  pnpm exec vitest run \
    src/auto-reply/reply/queue/persist.test.ts \
    src/auto-reply/reply/queue.followup-restart-roundtrip.test.ts \
    src/infra/state-migrations.test.ts \
    src/infra/followup-queue-sqlite.test.ts
  ```
- Evidence after fix: Copied live terminal output from real on-disk OpenClaw state (`openclaw.sqlite` I/O, no persist mocks):
  ```
  Terminal output:
  RUN  v4.1.7 /home/joseph/dev/openclaw
  Test Files  4 passed (4)
  Tests  33 passed (33)
  Head 8066e406c behaviors verified:
  - Telegram followup → SQLite → simulated restart → exactly-once redelivery
  - Doctor: live-chat-followup-queues.json → followup_queue_entries, sidecar removed
  - Fresh install: followup_queue_entries table on first openclaw.sqlite open
  - room_event + quoted-reply + bare-reset inbound context round-trip through restore
  ```
- Observed result after fix: After simulated restart, restored queue items retain prompt, routing, reply target, provenance, and inbound context; drain delivers the restored prompt once; post-drain persistence prevents replay on a second restart.
- What was not tested: Live native Telegram desktop capture (Mantis workflow requires write/admin on `openclaw/openclaw`; prior attempt blocked on contributor read permission).

target.title: Fix home path replacement in shortenHomeInString utility
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix `shortenHomeInString()` so it only replaces `display.home` when the match occurs at a real path boundary.
- Prevents incorrect shortening of home-like substrings such as `/srv/openclaw-home-other/dir`.
- Adds focused regression coverage for the boundary-aware helper in utils.test.ts.

## Why

- The previous implementation used unbounded substring replacement and could corrupt unrelated paths.
- This is a deterministic display formatting bug in the OpenClaw path helper.
- A real command invocation now proves the behavior outside Vitest.

## How

- Updated utils.ts:
  - `shortenHomeInString()` now scans for `display.home` and validates the preceding/following characters.
  - It only substitutes when the home token starts at the string or is bounded by a text delimiter, not when it appears inside another path segment.
- Added regression tests in utils.test.ts for:
  - a non-home-like path segment that must remain unchanged,
  - a true home-prefix path that still shortens correctly.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `shortenHomeInString()` was rewriting home-like substrings inside longer path segments, causing paths like `/srv/openclaw-home-other/dir` to be shortened incorrectly.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Windows, Node 25.9.0, using `npx --yes pnpm` and `npx --yes tsx`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  cd 'd:\Vs Code\AshyyHere\openclaw'
  npx --yes pnpm exec vitest run src/utils.test.ts --reporter=dot --no-color
  npx --yes tsx -e "import { shortenHomeInString } from './src/utils.ts'; console.log('/srv/openclaw-home-other/dir =>', shortenHomeInString('/srv/openclaw-home-other/dir'));"
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  17 passed (17)
  /srv/openclaw-home-other/dir => /srv/openclaw-home-other/dir
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** the helper preserves `/srv/openclaw-home-other/dir` unchanged, while still shortening valid home-prefixed paths.
- **What was not tested:** full OpenClaw release gating, live gateway/runtime behavior, or sibling terminal-core display surfaces outside this helper validation.

## Testing

- `npx --yes pnpm exec vitest run utils.test.ts --reporter=dot --no-color`
- Result: `Test Files  1 passed (1)` and `Tests  17 passed (17)`

## Checklist

- [x] Tests added/updated
- [x] Real behavior proof included
- [x] No schema/config change
- [x] Fix limited to display/path-formatting logic

target.title: feat: channel echo / session pinning
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds echo delivery infrastructure that mirrors session messages to additional channels
- `SessionEchoTarget` type on `SessionEntry` for persistent echo target configuration
- `message:sent` internal hook triggers echo delivery (fire-and-forget, no circular echo)
- CLI: `openclaw sessions echo add/remove/list` subcommands
- Gateway method: `sessions.echo` for programmatic pin/unpin

## Motivation

Sessions used across multiple transports (e.g. Telegram and the web UI) result in fragmented chat logs. Echo targets let every turn appear in all pinned channels regardless of originating transport.

## Files changed

| File | Purpose |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/sessions/types.ts` | `SessionEchoTarget` type + `echoTargets` field |
| `src/infra/outbound/echo.ts` | Core echo resolution + delivery |
| `src/infra/outbound/echo-hook.ts` | Internal hook registration |
| `src/commands/sessions-echo.ts` | CLI subcommands |
| `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts` | `sessions.echo` gateway method |
| `src/gateway/server-methods.ts` | Method registration |
| `src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts` | Hook auto-registration at boot |
| `src/cli/program/register.status-health-sessions.ts` | CLI command wiring |

## Test plan

- [ ] Unit test for `resolveEchoTargets` (deduplication, role filtering)
- [ ] Integration test: add echo target via gateway method, send message, verify echo arrives
- [ ] Verify circular echo prevention (echo delivery does not re-trigger hooks)
- [ ] CLI smoke test: add/list/remove echo targets

Supersedes #88386 (closed: branch had unrelated deployment commits).

---

## Proof (manual)

> Mantis isn't triggerable with my repo access, so this is supplied manually. Ran this branch live on a real Telegram bot (Windows, real session store). Covers the **integration**, **circular-echo**, and **CLI smoke** items in the test plan above. Telegram chat id redacted as `<chat>`.

**Build note:** the branch did not build/typecheck as-is — repaired in fd8ada2025 (now on this branch): duplicate `SessionsEchoParams`, stale `theme` import + `theme.bold`, `echoTargets` missing from the reserved slot-key list, and a wrong `.find`/`.filter` param annotation (`threadId` is `string|number`). `tsgo:core` clean after.

**1. CLI `add` / `list` / `remove`** (real store, net-zero):
```
$ openclaw sessions echo add  --session-key …:thread:<chat>:67 --channel telegram --to <chat> --thread-id 26237 --label echo-proof
  Added echo target: telegram -> <chat> (echo-proof)
$ openclaw sessions echo list --session-key …:thread:<chat>:67 --json
  { "echoTargets":[{ "channel":"telegram","to":"<chat>","threadId":"26237","label":"echo-proof","addedAt":… }] }
$ openclaw sessions echo remove --session-key …:thread:<chat>:67 --channel telegram --to <chat> --thread-id 26237
  Removed echo target: telegram -> <chat>
$ openclaw sessions echo list --session-key …:thread:<chat>:67
  No echo targets configured for this session.
```
Raw store reads confirmed `echoTargets: null → […] → null`.

**2. Live cross-channel mirror** (source thread 67 → pinned thread 26237):
```
[telegram] Inbound message …direct, 12 chars                            ← user message in thread 67
[telegram] outbound send ok messageId=36822 threadId=26237 silent=true  ← echoed to pinned thread
[telegram] outbound send ok messageId=36824 threadId=26237 silent=true  ← echoed
```
Echo fires off `message:received`, independent of the agent turn.

**3. No circular echo** — every echo delivery targets only the pinned thread with `silent=true`; none re-echoed. Structural guarantee: `fireEchoDeliveries()` omits `session`/`mirror` so the echo's own send never raises `message:sent`, so it cannot re-enter the hook (`echo.ts`).

---

## Update — surface-agnostic echo + live web→Telegram proof

Since the last review:
- **`sessions.echo.threadId` widened** to accept numeric thread IDs (Telegram forum threads arrive as numbers) + focused protocol coverage. (`20dba6e`)
- **Echo extended to the gateway chat path** so it fires for **control-ui / web / CLI** turns, not only channel-originated ones. A turn on a session with echo targets now mirrors **both** the inbound user message and the assistant reply to the tagged channel(s) — channel-agnostic, gated so channel-pipeline turns don't double-echo. (`73b6c75e` + the existing inline assistant-echo)

This realizes the design intent: **an echo target on a session mirrors that session's turns to the tagged thread no matter which surface resumes the session** (channel, web, or CLI), because every surface shares the same session key.

### Live proof — web app → Telegram (the original thread)
A message sent in a Telegram-originated session **from the web app** mirrored into the original Telegram thread:

![web to telegram echo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/echo88815-proof/echo-web-to-telegram-88815.png)

Gateway log (chat id redacted) — `echo-to-thread: 0 → 2` from one webchat turn:
```
telegram outbound send ok ... chatId=<chat> messageId=37041 threadId=67 silent=true   <- user message   (rendered "[via webchat]")
telegram outbound send ok ... chatId=<chat> messageId=37042 threadId=67 silent=true   <- assistant reply (rendered "[echo]")
cli turn ... outBytes=227 outHash=545140268128                                         <- real webchat turn (not a heartbeat)
```
Exactly two `silent=true` deliveries (user + assistant), no cascade — the no-loop guard held.

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Notes

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches
